AI Investigation Brief · Claude 3.5 Sonnet

87% CONFIDENCE
T1486 T1021 T1059.001

AI Investigation Pipeline — 3-Round Cross-Check DAC-18158
Tokenize
847 PII tokens generated
23ms · Vault: NS_DACT_*
Complete
→

Domain routing: SIEM, EDR, Firewall, TI
1.1s · 4 domains routed
Complete

Primary Investigation
Verdict: TRUE POSITIVE

Consensus: TRUE POSITIVE
Final confidence: 87% · 2.8s

→
Detokenize
Re-identified for analyst
RBAC: John Lim
19ms · Audit logged
Complete
Total pipeline time: 10.3s · 3/3 models agree · Consensus: TRUE POSITIVE · Privacy budget consumed: ε = 0.47 of 2.0
Reasoning Chain5 indicators
1
Mass file rename event — 847 files renamed from .docx/.xlsx to .enc extension within 43 seconds on host DACTASG-WS-047. This rate (19.7 files/sec) is consistent with automated ransomware encryption behavior.
2
Suspicious parent process chain — svchost.exe spawned by powershell.exe (PID 4821) is anomalous. Legitimate svchost is spawned exclusively by services.exe. This indicates process injection or masquerading.
3
Correlated lateral movement — DAC-18157 (Unusual Host Communication) fired 12 minutes prior on the same user account accessing ADMIN$ shares on 4 additional hosts. Indicative of pre-ransomware staging.
4
IOC match — SHA256 hash of powershell payload (a3f4b8c2...) matches known LockBit 3.0 dropper variant in VirusTotal with 43/68 vendor detections. MISP feed confirmation pending.
5
High-value target context — john.santos has Domain Admin privileges. The affected host contains Finance shared drives. Blast radius assessment: 12 additional hosts accessible via current credentials.
Counter-Evidence Considered3 factors
No network beacon/C2 communication detected to external IPs — could indicate offline encryption mode or blocked egress
User john.santos had IT maintenance scheduled for 13:00–14:00 SGT (per calendar), potentially legitimate bulk operations
Endpoint agent returned status "Active" (not isolated) — if true ransomware, shadow copy deletion expected but not yet observed
Enrichment Summary
Affected Host
DACTASG-WS-047

Manual

Manual
Client Escalation Draft
AI Generated DRAFT READY
To: d.sok@nagaworld.com (CISO)
CC: it-security@nagaworld.com, soc-lead@dacta.global
Subj: [P1] Potential Ransomware Activity — DAC-18158
Dear D. Sok and NagaWorld Security Team,

Dacta SG SOC has detected a critical security incident on your environment requiring immediate attention. Ransomware-like encryption activity has been observed on host DACTASG-WS-047 (Finance Dept). 847 files renamed .enc.

Summary of Findings:
• Automated file encryption detected at 13:21:44 SGT (19.7 files/sec)
• Extensions changed: .docx→.enc, .xlsx→.enc, .pdf→.enc
• Process chain: powershell.exe → svchost.exe (PID 4821 → 7842)
• Lateral movement to 4 additional hosts via ADMIN$ shares
• User account: john.santos (Domain Admin privileges)

Immediate Actions Taken:
• Alert correlated with DAC-18157 (Unusual Host Communication)
• Assigned P1 priority — SLA timer active
• AI investigation pipeline returned TRUE POSITIVE (87% confidence, 3/3 models agree)
• Host isolation pending your authorisation

Recommended Client Actions:
• Authorise immediate isolation of DACTASG-WS-047
• Notify Finance department of potential data impact
• Engage legal/compliance if data exfiltration confirmed

We will continue to provide updates as investigation progresses. Please respond to confirm receipt and isolation authorisation.

Regards,
Dacta SG SOC Team
soc-lead@dacta.global · +65 6XXX XXXX
Click body above to edit directly

title: Suspicious LSASS Memory Access via Non-Standard Process id: d8c9b2a1-4e7f-4a3b-8c1d-9f2e6b4a7d3c status: experimental description: | Detects access to LSASS process memory from non-standard processes that typically don't need credential access. Excludes known legitimate tools (AV, EDR agents). references: - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1003/001/ - https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/ author: Dacta SOC Team date: 2026/02/28 modified: 2026/02/28 tags: - attack.credential_access - attack.t1003.001 - detection.emerging_threats logsource: category: process_access product: windows detection: selection: TargetImage|endswith: '\lsass.exe' GrantedAccess|contains: - '0x1010' # PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED + VM_READ - '0x1410' # + PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION - '0x1F0FFF' # FULL_ACCESS filter_main: SourceImage|endswith: - '\MsMpEng.exe' - '\SentinelAgent.exe' - '\CylanceSvc.exe' condition: selection and not filter_main falsepositives: - Legitimate admin tools accessing LSASS level: high
Sigma YAML Lines: 35 UTF-8
✓ Valid syntax Confidence: 92%
Auto-Translate (Uncoder Engine)
process where event.category == "process_access" and TargetImage like~ "*\\lsass.exe" and GrantedAccess in ("0x1010", "0x1410", "0x1F0FFF") and not SourceImage in ( "*\\MsMpEng.exe", "*\\SentinelAgent.exe", "*\\CylanceSvc.exe" )

Detection Blind Spots34 gaps
T1134 — Access Token Manipulation
No rules covering token theft/impersonation via Windows APIs
T1037 — Boot/Logon Init Scripts
Missing startup folder monitoring and registry run key detection
T1534 — Internal Spearphishing
No correlation rule for internal email-based lateral movement
T1491 — Defacement
Web defacement detection requires HTTP response monitoring
T0800 — Activate Firmware Update Mode (ICS)
OT firmware manipulation — requires Modbus/OPC UA log source

 Hunt-to-Rule Graduation Criteria

            
Active hunt hypotheses are promoted to permanent detection rules when they meet ALL of the following:

            
    
              
  1. Minimum True Positives: ≥ 3 confirmed true positives across different client environments
    2. 
              
  2. False Positive Rate: < 5% over a 30-day observation window
    3. 
              
  3. MITRE Mapping: Technique must be mapped to a validated ATT&CK technique
    4. 
              
  4. Query Stability: Hunt query must execute successfully for 14+ consecutive days without modification
    5. 
              
  5. Peer Review: Reviewed and approved by a Senior Hunt Lead
    6. 
              
  6. Client Coverage: Successfully tested against ≥ 2 client environments (SOCaaS)
    7. 
            

            
Rules that graduate are auto-deployed to all applicable client SIEMs via the SIEM Push pipeline.

          


          
          

            
          


        


        
        


          

            
Pre-built hunt playbooks with structured methodology and step-by-step guidance.

Data Privacy Engine

            
PDPA-Compliant Multi-LLM Investigation Pipeline

          

          

            
            

              

              All Systems Operational
            

            
          

        


        
        

          

            
Records Tokenized Today

            
--

            
loading...

          

          

            
PII Detection Rate

            
--

            
loading...

          

          

            
Active Policies

            
--

            
loading...

          

          

            
Mean Tokenization Latency

            
--

            
loading...

          

        


        
        

          

            Privacy Pipeline — SIEM Investigation E2E Flow
            Query → SIEM Search → Tokenize → LLM Analysis → Detokenize Report → Analyst Review → Re-Tokenize → Store
          

          

            
            


              
              

                

                  
01

                  
Analyst Query

                  
SOC analyst triggers investigation with raw identifiers

                  
host:dc01.clientabc.local
user:"john.doe"

                  
Raw PII Query

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
02

                  
L1 — SIEM Search

                  
Query runs against Elastic/Splunk with real values — SIEM requires actual identifiers to match indexed data

                  
host:dc01.clientabc.local
user:"john.doe"
→ 847 matching events

                  
Encrypted Transport

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
03

                  
L2 — Tokenize Results

                  
All PII in SIEM results replaced with reversible tokens — from this point, no PII in the pipeline

                  
Client ABC → [[CLIENT_0x7A]]
john.doe → [[USER_0x3F]]
dc01 → [[HOST_001]]

                  
Vault-Secured

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
04

                  
L3 — 3-Round LLM

                  
Adversarial cross-check on tokenized data only — LLMs never see real PII

                  

                    
Claude Opus94%

                    
GPT 5.291%

                    
Gemini 3.1 Pro97%

                  

                  
Zero LLM Exposure

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
05

                  
L4 — Detokenize Report

                  
LLM findings restored with real identifiers for analyst review & action

                  
[[CLIENT_0x7A]] → Client ABC
[[USER_0x3F]] → john.doe
Human-readable report

                  
RBAC + MFA

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
06

                  
L5 — Analyst Review

                  
Authorized analyst reviews findings with real identifiers, confirms or rejects verdict

                  
Verdict: TP / FP / Suspicious
Analyst notes & escalation
RBAC-gated access

                  
Human-in-the-Loop

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
07

                  
L6 — Re-Tokenize Storage

                  
Final report re-tokenized before audit trail storage — data at rest contains no PII

                  
Data at rest = tokenized
Immutable audit trail

                  
Tamper-Proof

                

              


              


              
              

                

                  
✓

                  
Complete

                  
Full report delivered. Stored copy tokenized. E2E audit trail preserved.

                  
E2E Verified

                

              


            

          

        



        
        

          

            SOC Data Classification & Treatment Policy
            7 categories · auto-applied at ingestion
          

          

            

              
                

                  Data Category
                  Risk Level
                  Treatment
                  Example
                

              
              
                

                  Client Names & Domains
                   Critical
                  Tokenize (reversible)
                  Client ABC → [[CLIENT_0x7A]]
                

                

                  IP Addresses, Hostnames
                   Critical
                  Pseudonymize
                  192.168.1.45 → [[IP_0x3F]]
                

                

                  Email Addresses
                   Critical
                  Tokenize
                  john@client.com → [[EMAIL_0x12]]
                

                

                  MITRE ATT&CK TTPs
                   Safe
                  Pass Through
                  T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact
                

                

                  File Hashes (SHA256)
                   Safe
                  Pass Through
                  a1b2c3d4e5f6...
                

                

                  Timestamps
                   Moderate
                  Generalise → Restore
                  14:23:07 → 14:00-15:00 window
                

                

                  Raw Log Payloads
                   Critical
                  Tokenize all identifiers
                  Keep structural patterns only

            Today
          

          

            

              
              

                
--

                
Today

Email (--)

Attack Timeline — Detailed with MITRE Mapping

                  

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 01:14 SGT

                        
Spear-phishing Email Delivered

                        
Email from no-reply@secure-doc-share.com to finance@nagaworld.com. Subject: "Urgent: Q4 Audit Document Review". Contains malicious Word macro attachment.

                        T1566.001 — Spearphishing Attachment
                      

                    

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 01:47 SGT

                        
Macro Execution — Cobalt Strike Dropper

                        
User enabled macros. VBA script downloaded stage-1 payload from hxxps://updates.typo3-cdn.net/kb/update.exe. Cobalt Strike beacon established on NAGA-WS-012.

                        T1059.005 — Visual Basic T1105 — Ingress Tool Transfer
                      

                    

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 02:03 SGT

                        
Persistence via Scheduled Task

                        
Scheduled task "MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA" created under SYSTEM. Beacon restarts every 15 minutes on NAGA-WS-012.

                        T1053.005 — Scheduled Task/Job
                      

                    

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 03:55 SGT

                        
Lateral Movement via PsExec to Domain Controller

                        
Attacker used harvested domain admin credentials (svc_backup) to PsExec into NAGA-DC01. Cobalt Strike beacon deployed on DC.

                        T1021.002 — SMB/Windows Admin Shares
                      

                    

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 09:47 SGT

                        
Ransomware Deployment — ALPHV/BlackCat

                        
ALPHV ransomware binary deployed via GPO across 847 endpoints. Encryption of .docx, .xlsx, .pdf, .db files. Extension changed to .alphv_enc. Ransom note dropped to C:\Users\Public\.

                        T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact T1490 — Inhibit System Recovery
                      

                    

                    

                      

                      

                        
2026-02-28 10:15 SGT

                        
DACTA Containment Initiated

                        
CrowdStrike Network Containment activated on 23 high-risk hosts. Firewall rules blocking C2 IPs pushed. DACTA War Room activated, WR-2026-003 opened.

                        RESPONSE ACTION
                      

                    

                  


                  
                  
Analyst Notebook

All containment actions require client authorization. Authorized by: D. Sok (NagaWorld CISO)

                    

                      
                      
                    

                  

                  

                    

                      
#ActionStatusPriorityAssignedAuthorized ByTimestamp

                      
                        
CA-001Network Isolation — 23 hosts via CrowdStrikeCompletedCriticalK. RasmussenD. Sok10:18 SGT

                        
CA-002Block C2 IPs (185.220.101.47, 91.108.4.180) at perimeter FWCompletedCriticalK. RasmussenD. Sok10:22 SGT

                        
CA-003Disable svc_backup domain accountCompletedCriticalP. TranD. Sok10:35 SGT

                        
CA-004Reset all privileged account passwords (DA, EA)In ProgressCriticalS. LimD. Sok11:00 SGT

                        
CA-005Block mega.nz egress at proxy (Zscaler)CompletedHighK. RasmussenD. Sok11:14 SGT

                        
CA-006Revoke all active VPN sessions & enforce MFA re-enrollmentApprovedHighM. NguyenPending—

                        
CA-007Enable enhanced logging on all DCs (Audit Policy GPO)PlannedMediumJ. Wang——

                      
                    

                  


                  
                  
Decision Matrix

                  

                    

                      
Network Isolation Criteria

                      

                         Active C2 beaconing detected

                         Ransomware binary executed

                         Lateral movement from host

                         Encryption in progress

                         Credential dumping activity
                      

                    

                    

                      
Account Disable Criteria

                      

                         Account used for lateral movement

                         Credentials in attacker hands (confirmed)

                         Unusual logon patterns post-incident

                         Service account with unusual auth

                         DA/EA accounts — immediate escalation
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    
 Eradication must only begin after full scope is confirmed. Phase 4 (Investigate) still in progress.

                    
                  


                  

                    
 Persistence Mechanisms

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1053.005 — Scheduled Task

                        
Remove "MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA" scheduled task from all affected hosts

                      

                      Done
                    

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1547.001 — Registry Run Keys

                        
Scan and remove any persistence in HKLM/HKCU Run keys matching Cobalt Strike beacon signatures

                      

                      Open
                    

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1505.003 — Web Shell

                        
Check IIS servers (NAGA-SRV-01, NAGA-SRV-12) for web shell deployment by attacker

                      

                      Pending
                    

                  


                  

                    
 Credential Cleanup

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1003.001 — LSASS Memory Dump

                        
Reset all domain admin, enterprise admin, and service account passwords. Force Kerberos ticket invalidation (krbtgt reset x2)

                      

                      Done
                    

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1558.001 — Golden Ticket

                        
Assess if attacker created Golden Ticket. If confirmed: reset krbtgt twice with 10h gap, force re-auth of all domain accounts

                      

                      Open
                    

                    

                      
                      

                        
T1078 — Valid Accounts

                        
Audit all service accounts for unnecessary privileges. Implement least-privilege (tiering model)

                      

                      Pending
                    

                  


                  

                    
 Recovery Tracking

                    

                      

                        
AssetMethodStatusEst. Recovery

                        
                          
NAGA-WS-012Reimage from gold imageIn Progress2h

                          
NAGA-DC02Restore from Veeam backup (2026-02-27)Pending4h

                          
NAGA-SRV-01Restore from Veeam backup (2026-02-27)Pending6h

                          
NAGA-WS-047Reimage from gold imagePending2h

                        
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    
 Executive Summary (1-2 page non-technical brief)

                    

                      On 28 February 2026, NagaWorld Ltd. experienced a ransomware attack attributed to the ALPHV/BlackCat threat actor group. The attack originated via a targeted spear-phishing email and resulted in the encryption of files across 847 endpoints. DACTA's MDR team detected the incident and activated War Room WR-2026-003 within 28 minutes of ransomware deployment. Containment actions isolated 23 high-risk systems within 45 minutes. Investigation is ongoing; recovery timeline estimated at 36-48 hours.
                    

                    

                      
                      
                      
                    

                  

                  

                    
 Technical Report

                    
Detailed forensic analysis, kill chain reconstruction, MITRE mapping, and remediation evidence. Auto-populates from investigation data.

                    

                      
                      
                    

                  

                  

                    
 IOC Export

                    
Export 47 IOCs for blocking, sharing, and threat intelligence integration.

                    

                      
                      
                      
                    

                  

                  

                    
 Lessons Learned

                    
Post-incident analysis to be completed within 5 business days.

                War Room Chat
                ❯
              

              

                
                

                  
D. Sok (NagaWorld)

                  
Can you confirm the affected subnet? We need to notify our network team.

                

                

                  
John Lim (Dacta)

                  
Confirmed — 10.10.4.0/24, isolating now. Finance VLAN. We're also seeing activity on 10.10.5.12.

                

                

                  
Copilot

                  War Room WR-2026-003 — NagaWorld Ransomware. Currently in Phase 4: Investigate. I've analyzed the available telemetry. You're tracking ALPHV/BlackCat with confirmed C2 at 185.220.101.47. 47 IOCs confirmed.
                

                

                  
Copilot

                  Immediate priority: Assess potential Golden Ticket creation given the DC compromise. svc_backup had DA rights — full krbtgt reset cycle is recommended.
                

              

              

                
Suggested for Phase 4

                Client Log Source Management
                
Per-client SIEM index patterns, health monitoring, and ingestion status — loaded from SIEMLess DB

              

              

                Client:

—
sources

—
sources

—
sources

events/day

            


            
            

              

                
                

                  
Monitoring Gap Detected

                  

                

              

            


            
            

              

                
                
Select a client organization

                
Choose a client to view their monitored log sources, health status, and ingestion metrics.

              

            

          

        


        
        

        

          

            

              Client Connectors
              
Per-client tech stack connectors — SIEM, EDR, Firewall, Threat Intel, and more


              

                

                  SIEM — Log search & correlation
                

                

                  EDR — Host query, isolation, containment
                

                

                  Firewall — Block/allow, responsive actions
                

                

                  Threat Intel — IOC enrichment & lookups
                

              

              

                
                Connectors that interact with SIEMLess via API — different clients may use different vendors. Log source SIEM indices are managed in Client Log Sources.
              

              
 — loaded from SIEMLess DB

            

            

              Client:
              
            

          

          

          

        


        


        
        

        

          

            

              AI Investigation RAG Router
              
Severity-gated retrieval pipeline — controls depth, model tier, and domain scope per alert priority

            

            Live Config
          

          

            

              
P1 / P0
Critical
Full RAG Pipeline

              
Alert Ingest→3-Round Redaction→Classifier (Haiku)→All 7 Domain Handlers→Context Aggregator→Reasoner (Opus)→Investigation Brief

              
ModelClaude Opus
Latency~18s
Domains7 / 7
TriggerAuto on alert

            

            

              
P2
High
Selective RAG

              
Alert Ingest→3-Round Redaction→Classifier (Haiku)→TI + SIEM Handlers→Reasoner (Sonnet)→Summary Card

              
ModelClaude Sonnet
Latency~8s
Domains2 / 7
TriggerAuto on assign

            

            

              
P3
Medium
Lightweight Enrichment

              
Alert Ingest→Redaction (R1 only)→KB Cache Lookup→TI Enrichment→Context Card

              
ModelHaiku (classifier)
Latency~3s
Domains1 / 7
TriggerOn analyst open

            

            

              
P4
Info / Low
Rule-Based Only

              
Alert Ingest→Rule Engine→Asset Tag→Auto-Close / Queue

              
ModelNone (rule-based)
Latency<1s
Domains0 / 7
TriggerAuto on ingest

            

          

          

            
            Analysts can trigger Full RAG on any severity via ⌘K Command Bar or the Run AI button on any alert card.
            On-Demand
          

        

        


        
        


          
          

            

              

                CLIENT:

                    SLA Configuration
                    
Client-specific response & resolution targets (overrides global defaults)

                  

                  

                    
                  

                

                

                  

                    P1
                    
Response / Resolution

                    
                    /
                    
                  

                  

                    P2
                    
Response / Resolution

                    
                    /
                    
                  

                  

                    P3
                    
Response / Resolution

                    
                    /
                    
                  

                  

                    P4
                    
Response / Resolution

                    
                    /
                    
                  

                

                

                  
                  Using global SLA defaults. Enable "Use Custom SLA" to set client-specific targets.
                

              


              
              

                

                  

                    Escalation Tiers
                    
Define escalation path — auto-escalate when SLA thresholds are breached

                  

                

                

                  
                  

                    

                      

                        L1
                        SOC Analyst (Initial Response)
                      

                      Auto-assigned
                    

                    

                      
ESCALATE TO L2 AFTER

                      
NOTIFY CONTACTS (TIER)

                    

                  

                  
                  

                    

                      

                        L2
                        Senior Analyst / Team Lead
                      

                      On SLA breach
                    

                    

                      
ESCALATE TO L3 AFTER

                      
NOTIFY CONTACTS (TIER)

                    

                  

                  
                  

                    

                      

                        L3
                        SOC Manager / Client CISO
                      

                      Critical escalation
                    

                    

                      
ESCALATE TO MGMT AFTER

                      
NOTIFY CONTACTS (TIER)

                    

                  

                

              

            


          

        



        
        

          

            
Investigation Decision LogCompliance

            
Every LLM-driven investigation decision is logged with: ticket ID, model used, verdict, confidence, analyst override (if any), and timestamp. This creates an immutable audit trail for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance.

            

              
Log all AI investigation decisions
Verdict, confidence, model, duration

              
Track analyst overrides
Who, when, justification

              
Rule version history
Detection rules + RAG config changes

              
Feedback loop telemetry
Analyst ratings feed into prompt tuning

              
              

            

          

          

            
Data Retention

            

              
Investigation Cache TTL

              
Audit Log Retention

              
              

            

          

        


        
        

        

          

            
LLM ConfigurationAI Engine

            

              
Primary Model (Investigation Briefs)

              
Fast Model (Enrichment, Triage)

              
Threat Intel Model

              
Monthly AI Budget Cap

              
Auto-run AI on all P1/P2 alerts

              
              

            

          

          

            
Access ControlSecurity

            

              
Require MFA for all analyst logins
Enforced via SSO provider

              
Inactivity Session Timeout
Auto-logout after idle period

              
Allowed IP Ranges

              
              

            

          

          

            
Notification Settings

            

              
P1 Alert — Slack + SMS
#soc-critical channel

              
Shift Handover Reminder
30 min before shift end

              
SLA Breach Warning
When 80% of SLA window elapsed

              
Daily Digest Email
08:00 SGT every morning

              
              

            

          

          

            
Tenant Settings

            

              
Organization Name

              
Timezone

              

                
Encryption: AES-256-GCM at rest & in transit

                
Data Pipeline: 3-Round Redaction for PII/PHI compliance

                
Data Residency: Singapore (SG) — PDPA compliant

              

              
Data Retention Policy

              
Ticket ID Prefix

              
              

            

          

        


        
        

          
SSO / SAML AuthenticationIdentity

          

            

              
Enable SSO Login
Show SSO buttons on login page. Email/password remains as break glass.

                Note: SSO/SAML providers must also be configured in the authentication backend. Domain-based routing requires the IdP metadata URL to be registered. Contact platform admin for setup.
              

            

          

        


        

      



      
      

        

          

            
Data Architecture

            
Dual-model ingestion — SOCaaS & MDR-only clients

          

          

            
          

        


        


          
          

            

              
Active Clients

              
12

              
Across both models

            

            

              
SOCaaS Clients

              
5

              
With Elastic SIEM

            

            

              
MDR-Only Clients

              
7

              
EDR / XDR / Email direct

            

            

              
Alert Bus

              
Jira

              
Single Source of Truth

            

          


          
          

            
Dual-Model Data Flow — SOCaaS (Model A) & MDR-only (Model B)


            
            


              
              


                
                

                  
MODEL A — SOCaaS (Full SIEM)


                  
                  

                    
 Full Log Sources

                    
Firewall, EDR, Identity, OS, Cloud, Email

                    
5 clients

                  


                  
                  

                    

                    

                  


                  
                  

                    

                      

                      

                        
Elastic SIEM

                        
DACTA Managed Detection

                      

                    

                    

                      

                        

                        3,645 EPS  ·  600+ rules  ·  ECS
                      

                      
Detects, correlates, and enriches events → creates Jira tickets on alert fire.

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  
MODEL B — MDR-only (No SIEM)


                  
                  

                    
 EDR / XDR Alerts

                    
CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Defender, Trend, Heimdal

                    
Direct alert feed — no SIEM

                    
7 clients

                  


                  
                  

                    
 Email Security

                    
Mimecast, Proofpoint, M365 Defender

                    
Phishing, malware, BEC alerts

                  

                


              


              
              

                
                

                  

                  
›

                

                
                

                
                

                  

                  
›

                

              


              
              

                

                  

                    

                    

                      
Jira Service Management

                      
Universal Alert Bus

                    

                  


                  

                    

                    All Alerts Converge Here
                  


                  

                    
Single Source of Truth

                    Ticket creation  ·  SLA assignment

                    Priority mapping  ·  Analyst routing


                    Both Model A and Model B clients funnel all detection alerts through Jira before reaching MCC.
                  

                

              


              
              

                

                  
                  

                    

                    
›

                  

                  
                  

                    
Jira ↔ MCC

                    
Every 30s

                  

                  
                  

                    
 sync

                    

                  

                

              


              
              

                

                  

                    

                    

                      
Mission Control Center

                      
For Blue Team, By Blue Team

                    

                  


                  
                  

                    

                      
Alert Feed

                      PRIMARY
                    

                    
All alerts via Jira sync

                    
Powers Dashboard, Triage Queue, metrics, and SLA timers. Both model clients unified here.

                  


                  
                  

                    

                      
Investigation Drill-Down

                      ON-DEMAND
                    

                    
MCC → Client EDR/XDR API (direct)

                    
Bypasses Jira for deep telemetry: process trees, network connections, file details. ~3–5s per lookup.

                  


                  
                  

                    

                      
AI / RAG Pipeline

                      ENRICHMENT
                    

                    
Severity-gated RAG Router

                    
Queries TI, KB, and entity stores via pgvector SQL for investigation context and AI Copilot.

                  


                  
                  

                    

                      
Workflow Sync

                      BI-DIRECTIONAL
                    

                    
Jira ↔ MCC (every 30s)

                    
Status updates, comments, and resolution flow back to Jira for client visibility.

                  

                

              


            


            
            

              ↩
              ON-DEMAND EDR QUERY
              MCC queries client EDR/XDR APIs directly during investigation drill-down — regardless of model (A or B). Bypasses Jira for deep telemetry.
            


          


          
          

            
⇄ Integration Flow Reference

            

              
                

                  Data Flow
                  Model
                  Direction
                  Protocol
                  Frequency
                  Latency
                

              
              
                

                  Client Logs → Elastic SIEM
                  A · SOCaaS
                   Push (Agent/Syslog)
                  Beats / Syslog
                  Continuous
                  Real-time
                

                

                  Elastic → Jira (Alert Ticket)
                  A · SOCaaS
                   Push (Webhook)
                  HTTPS REST
                  On detection
                  <3s
                

                

                  EDR/XDR → Jira (Alert Ticket)
                  B · MDR-only
                   Push (Webhook)
                  HTTPS REST
                  On alert fire
                  <5s
                

                

                  Email Security → Jira
                  B · MDR-only
                   Push (Webhook)
                  HTTPS REST
                  On detection
                  <5s
                

                

                  Jira → MCC (Alert Sync)
                  Both
                  ↓ Pull (Polling)
                  REST API
                  Every 30s
                  <5s
                

                

                  MCC → Client EDR (Investigation)
                  Both
                  ↓ Pull (On-Demand)
                  HTTPS API
                  Per investigation
                  ~3–5s
                

                

                  MCC ↔ Jira (Workflow Sync)
                  Both
                  ↕ Bi-directional
                  REST API
                  Every 30s
                  <5s
                

                

                  TI Sources → MCC
                  Both
                  ↓ Pull (Scheduled)
                  TAXII / REST
                  Every 15min
                  N/A
                

                

                  MCC → DACTA KB (RAG)
                  Both
                   Internal Query
                  pgvector SQL
                  Per AI invocation
                  ~1s
